member.name: T:Csv.CsvBufferWriter
member.summary: High-performance, memory-pool-based CSV writer that minimizes allocations.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvBufferWriter.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of CsvBufferWriter with default options.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvBufferWriter.#ctor(Csv.CsvMemoryOptions)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of CsvBufferWriter with specified options.
member.param: The memory options to use.

member.name: P:Csv.CsvBufferWriter.WrittenCount
member.summary: Gets the total number of characters written.

member.name: P:Csv.CsvBufferWriter.Capacity
member.summary: Gets the current capacity of the writer.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvBufferWriter.WriteCsv(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Char}},System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Char}[]},System.Char,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Writes CSV data using ReadOnlyMemory for headers and rows.
member.param: The CSV headers.
The CSV data rows.
The column separator character.
Whether to skip writing the header row.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvBufferWriter.WriteRow(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Char}},System.Char)
member.summary: Writes a single CSV row.
member.param: The row cells.
The column separator character.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvBufferWriter.WriteCell(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char},System.Char)
member.summary: Writes a single cell with proper CSV escaping.
member.param: The cell content.
The column separator character.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvBufferWriter.Write(System.Char)
member.summary: Writes a single character.
member.param: The character to write.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvBufferWriter.Write(System.String)
member.summary: Writes a string.
member.param: The string to write.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvBufferWriter.Write(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char})
member.summary: Writes a span of characters.
member.param: The span to write.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvBufferWriter.GetSpan(System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets a span to write to with at least the specified size.
member.param: The minimum size required.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvBufferWriter.GetMemory(System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets memory to write to with at least the specified size.
member.param: The minimum size required.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvBufferWriter.Advance(System.Int32)
member.summary: Advances the position after writing the specified number of characters.
member.param: The number of characters written.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvBufferWriter.ToString
member.summary: Converts the written content to a string.
member.returns: The CSV content as a string.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvBufferWriter.CopyTo(System.Span{System.Char})
member.summary: Copies the written content to the specified span.
member.param: The destination span.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvBufferWriter.EnsureCapacity(System.Int32)
member.summary: Ensures the writer has capacity for at least the specified number of additional characters.
member.param: The minimum additional capacity required.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvBufferWriter.Dispose
member.summary: Disposes the writer and returns buffers to the pool.

member.name: T:Csv.CsvLineSplitter
member.summary: Splits a single line (multiline handling is done independently) into multiple parts

member.name: T:Csv.CsvMemoryOptions
member.summary: Configuration options for memory-efficient CSV operations using pooled buffers.

member.name: P:Csv.CsvMemoryOptions.MemoryPool
member.summary: Gets or sets the memory pool to use for buffer allocation. If null, uses the default ArrayPool.

member.name: P:Csv.CsvMemoryOptions.InitialBufferSize
member.summary: Gets or sets the initial buffer size for pooled operations. Default is 4096 characters.

member.name: P:Csv.CsvMemoryOptions.ReuseBuffers
member.summary: Gets or sets whether to reuse buffers across operations. Default is true.

member.name: P:Csv.CsvMemoryOptions.MaxBufferSize
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum buffer size that can be allocated. Default is 1MB (524,288 characters).

member.name: P:Csv.CsvMemoryOptions.UseVectorization
member.summary: Gets or sets whether to use vectorized operations when available. Default is true.

member.name: P:Csv.CsvMemoryOptions.StreamingThreshold
member.summary: Gets or sets the threshold for switching to streaming mode for large data. Default is 64KB.

member.name: P:Csv.CsvMemoryOptions.EnableZeroCopy
member.summary: Gets or sets whether to enable zero-copy parsing when possible. Default is true.

member.name: P:Csv.CsvMemoryOptions.ClearBuffers
member.summary: Gets or sets whether to clear rented buffers for security. Default is false for performance.

member.name: P:Csv.CsvMemoryOptions.DirectAllocationThreshold
member.summary: Gets or sets the threshold below which direct allocation is used instead of buffer pooling. This reduces memory overhead for small datasets. Default is 2048 characters.

member.name: P:Csv.CsvMemoryOptions.CharArrayPool
member.summary: Gets the ArrayPool instance to use for char buffer allocation.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvMemoryOptions.Clone
member.summary: Creates a copy of the current options.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvMemoryOptions.Validate
member.summary: Validates the current configuration and throws if invalid.

member.name: T:Csv.CsvOptions
member.summary: Defines the options that can be passed to customize the reading or writing of csv files.
member.remarks: Do not reuse an instance of for multiple reads or writes.

member.name: P:Csv.CsvOptions.RowsToSkip
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of rows to skip before reading the header row, defaults to 0.

member.name: P:Csv.CsvOptions.SkipRow
member.summary: Gets or sets a function to skip the current row based on its raw string value or 1-based index. Skips empty rows and rows starting with # by default.

member.name: P:Csv.CsvOptions.Separator
member.summary: Gets or sets the character to use for separating data, defaults to '\0' which will auto-detect from the header row.

member.name: P:Csv.CsvOptions.TrimData
member.summary: Gets or sets whether data should be trimmed when accessed, defaults to false.

member.name: P:Csv.CsvOptions.Comparer
member.summary: Gets or sets the comparer to use when looking up header names.

member.name: P:Csv.CsvOptions.HeaderMode
member.summary: Gets or sets an indicator to the parser to expect a header row or not, defaults to .

member.name: P:Csv.CsvOptions.ValidateColumnCount
member.summary: Gets or sets whether a row should be validated immediately that the column count matches the header count, defaults to false.

member.name: P:Csv.CsvOptions.ReturnEmptyForMissingColumn
member.summary: Gets or sets whether an empty string is returned for a missing column, defaults to false.

member.name: P:Csv.CsvOptions.Aliases
member.summary: Can be used to use multiple names for a single column. (e.g. to allow "CategoryName", "Category Name", "Category-Name")
member.remarks: A group with no matches is ignored.

member.name: P:Csv.CsvOptions.AllowNewLineInEnclosedFieldValues
member.summary: Respects new line (either \r\n or \n) characters inside field values enclosed in double quotes, defaults to false.

member.name: P:Csv.CsvOptions.AllowBackSlashToEscapeQuote
member.summary: Allows the sequence "\"" to be a valid quoted value (in addition to the standard """"), defaults to false.

member.name: P:Csv.CsvOptions.AllowSingleQuoteToEncloseFieldValues
member.summary: Allows the single-quote character to be used to enclose field values, defaults to false.

member.name: P:Csv.CsvOptions.AllowEnclosedFieldValues
member.summary: Allows field values to be enclosed in quotes. When set to false quotes will be treated as normal characters, defaults to true.

member.name: P:Csv.CsvOptions.AutoRenameHeaders
member.summary: Automatically renames duplicate headers by appending a number (e.g., "A", "A2", "A3"). Empty headers are renamed to "Empty", "Empty2", etc. Defaults to true. When set to false, duplicate headers will throw an InvalidOperationException.

member.name: P:Csv.CsvOptions.NewLine
member.summary: The new line string to use when multiline field values are read, defaults to .
member.remarks: Requires to be set to true for this to have any effect.

member.name: T:Csv.CsvReader
member.summary: Helper class to read csv (comma separated values) data.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvReader.Read(System.IO.TextReader,Csv.CsvOptions)
member.summary: Reads the lines from the reader.
member.param: The text reader to read the data from.
The optional options to use when reading.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvReader.ReadFromStream(System.IO.Stream,Csv.CsvOptions)
member.summary: Reads the lines from the stream.
member.param: The stream to read the data from.
The optional options to use when reading.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvReader.ReadFromText(System.String,Csv.CsvOptions)
member.summary: Reads the lines from the csv string.
member.param: The csv string to read the data from.
The optional options to use when reading.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvReader.ReadAsSpan(System.IO.TextReader,Csv.CsvOptions)
member.summary: Reads the lines from the reader with enhanced Span/Memory support.
member.param: The text reader to read the data from.
The optional options to use when reading.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvReader.ReadFromStreamAsSpan(System.IO.Stream,Csv.CsvOptions)
member.summary: Reads the lines from the stream with enhanced Span/Memory support.
member.param: The stream to read the data from.
The optional options to use when reading.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvReader.ReadFromTextAsSpan(System.String,Csv.CsvOptions)
member.summary: Reads the lines from the csv string with enhanced Span/Memory support.
member.param: The csv string to read the data from.
The optional options to use when reading.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvReader.ReadFromMemoryOptimized(System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Char},Csv.CsvOptions,Csv.CsvMemoryOptions)
member.summary: Reads CSV data from memory with enhanced memory management options.
member.param: The CSV data as ReadOnlyMemory.
The CSV parsing options.
The memory management options.
member.returns: An enumerable of CSV lines with memory optimization.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvReader.CreateBufferWriter(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.String},System.Char,Csv.CsvMemoryOptions)
member.summary: Creates a buffer writer for optimized CSV writing.
member.param: The CSV headers.
The column separator.
The memory options.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvReader.ReadAsync(System.IO.TextReader,Csv.CsvOptions)
member.summary: Reads the lines from the reader.
member.param: The text reader to read the data from.
The optional options to use when reading.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvReader.ReadFromStreamAsync(System.IO.Stream,Csv.CsvOptions)
member.summary: Reads the lines from the stream.
member.param: The stream to read the data from.
The optional options to use when reading.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvReader.ReadFromTextAsync(System.String,Csv.CsvOptions)
member.summary: Reads the lines from the csv string.
member.param: The csv string to read the data from.
The optional options to use when reading.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvReader.GetColumn``1(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Csv.ICsvLine},System.Int32,System.Func{System.String,``0})
member.summary: Gets a single column from the entire Enumeration of `ICsvLine`
member.param: The enumeration of `ICsvLine`
The index (starting from 0) of the the column to extract
The transformation function to parse from the string values
member.typeparam: The datatype to transform the string inputs into
member.returns: An enumeration of the transformed values of the selected column

member.name: M:Csv.CsvReader.GetColumn(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Csv.ICsvLine},System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets a single column from the entire Enumeration of `ICsvLine`
member.param: The enumeration of `ICsvLine`
The index (starting from 0) of the the column to extract
member.returns: An enumerations of the string values of the selected column

member.name: M:Csv.CsvReader.GetBlock(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Csv.ICsvLine},System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets a range/block of values from the given enumeration of `ICsvLine`
member.param: The enumeration of `ICsvLine`
The index(starting from 0) of the rows to start the capture of. Default value is -1
The number of rows to capture from the start row. If the default value (or any negative number) is passed, selects all the rows till the end
The index(starting from 0) of all the columns to start the capture of.
The number of rows to capture from the start column. If the default value (or any negative number) is passed, selects all the rows till the end

member.name: M:Csv.CsvReader.ReadFromMemory(System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Char},Csv.CsvOptions)
member.summary: Reads the lines from the csv string.
member.param: The csv string to read the data from.
The optional options to use when reading.

member.name: T:Csv.CsvWriter
member.summary: Helper class to write csv (comma separated values) data.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvWriter.Write(System.IO.TextWriter,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String[]},System.Char)
member.summary: Writes the lines to the writer without headers. Column count is determined from the first data line.
member.param: The text writer to write the data to.
The lines with data that should be written.
The separator to use between columns (comma, semicolon, tab, ...)

member.name: M:Csv.CsvWriter.Write(System.IO.TextWriter,System.String[],System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String[]},System.Char,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Writes the lines to the writer.
member.param: The text writer to write the data to.
The headers that should be used for the first line, determines the number of columns. Can be null if skipHeaderRow is true.
The lines with data that should be written.
The separator to use between columns (comma, semicolon, tab, ...)
Indicate whether the header row should be skipped, defaults to false.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvWriter.WriteToText(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String[]},System.Char)
member.summary: Writes the lines without headers and returns the result. Column count is determined from the first data line.
member.param: The lines with data that should be written.
The separator to use between columns (comma, semicolon, tab, ...)

member.name: M:Csv.CsvWriter.WriteToText(System.String[],System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String[]},System.Char,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Writes the lines and return the result.
member.param: The headers that should be used for the first line, determines the number of columns. Can be null if skipHeaderRow is true.
The lines with data that should be written.
The separator to use between columns (comma, semicolon, tab, ...)
Indicate whether the header row should be skipped, defaults to false.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvWriter.Write(System.IO.TextWriter,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Char}[]},System.Char)
member.summary: Writes the lines to the writer without headers using memory-efficient ReadOnlyMemory<char> arrays. Column count is determined from the first data line.
member.param: The text writer to write the data to.
The lines with data that should be written.
The separator to use between columns (comma, semicolon, tab, ...)

member.name: M:Csv.CsvWriter.Write(System.IO.TextWriter,System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Char}[],System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Char}[]},System.Char,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Writes the lines to the writer using memory-efficient ReadOnlyMemory<char> arrays.
member.param: The text writer to write the data to.
The headers that should be used for the first line, determines the number of columns. Can be null if skipHeaderRow is true.
The lines with data that should be written.
The separator to use between columns (comma, semicolon, tab, ...)
Indicate whether the header row should be skipped, defaults to false.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvWriter.Write(System.IO.TextWriter,System.ReadOnlySpan{System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Char}},System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Char}[]},System.Char,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Writes the lines to the writer using memory-efficient ReadOnlySpan<char> headers.
member.param: The text writer to write the data to.
The headers that should be used for the first line, determines the number of columns. Can be empty if skipHeaderRow is true.
The lines with data that should be written.
The separator to use between columns (comma, semicolon, tab, ...)
Indicate whether the header row should be skipped, defaults to false.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvWriter.WriteToText(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Char}[]},System.Char)
member.summary: Writes the lines without headers and returns the result using memory-efficient operations. Column count is determined from the first data line.
member.param: The lines with data that should be written.
The separator to use between columns (comma, semicolon, tab, ...)

member.name: M:Csv.CsvWriter.WriteToText(System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Char}[],System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Char}[]},System.Char,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Writes the lines and return the result using memory-efficient operations.
member.param: The headers that should be used for the first line, determines the number of columns. Can be null if skipHeaderRow is true.
The lines with data that should be written.
The separator to use between columns (comma, semicolon, tab, ...)
Indicate whether the header row should be skipped, defaults to false.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvWriter.WriteToBuffer(System.Span{System.Char},System.ReadOnlySpan{System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Char}},System.ReadOnlySpan{System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Char}[]},System.Char,System.Int32@,System.Boolean)
member.summary: High-performance buffer-based writing with pre-allocated destination buffer.
member.param: The destination buffer to write to.
The headers for the CSV.
The data lines to write.
The separator to use between columns.
The number of characters written to the buffer.
Indicate whether the header row should be skipped, defaults to false.
member.returns: True if all data was written successfully, false if buffer was too small.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvWriter.WriteAsync(System.IO.TextWriter,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String[]},System.Char,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously writes the lines to the writer without headers. Column count is determined from the first data line.
member.param: The text writer to write the data to.
The lines with data that should be written.
The separator to use between columns (comma, semicolon, tab, ...)
The cancellation token to use.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvWriter.WriteAsync(System.IO.TextWriter,System.String[],System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String[]},System.Char,System.Boolean,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously writes the lines to the writer.
member.param: The text writer to write the data to.
The headers that should be used for the first line, determines the number of columns. Can be null if skipHeaderRow is true.
The lines with data that should be written.
The separator to use between columns (comma, semicolon, tab, ...)
Indicate whether the header row should be skipped, defaults to false.
The cancellation token to use.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvWriter.WriteAsync(System.IO.TextWriter,System.Collections.Generic.IAsyncEnumerable{System.String[]},System.Char)
member.summary: Asynchronously writes the lines to the writer without headers. Column count is determined from the first data line.
member.param: The text writer to write the data to.
The lines with data that should be written.
The separator to use between columns (comma, semicolon, tab, ...)

member.name: M:Csv.CsvWriter.WriteAsync(System.IO.TextWriter,System.String[],System.Collections.Generic.IAsyncEnumerable{System.String[]},System.Char,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Writes the lines to the writer.
member.param: The text writer to write the data to.
The headers that should be used for the first line, determines the number of columns. Can be null if skipHeaderRow is true.
The lines with data that should be written.
The separator to use between columns (comma, semicolon, tab, ...)
Indicate whether the header row should be skipped, defaults to false.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvWriter.WriteAsync(System.IO.TextWriter,System.String[],System.Collections.Generic.IAsyncEnumerable{System.String[]},System.Char,System.Boolean,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Writes the lines to the writer.
member.param: The text writer to write the data to.
The headers that should be used for the first line, determines the number of columns. Can be null if skipHeaderRow is true.
The lines with data that should be written.
The separator to use between columns (comma, semicolon, tab, ...)
Indicate whether the header row should be skipped, defaults to false.
The cancellation token to use.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvWriter.WriteToTextAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IAsyncEnumerable{System.String[]},System.Char)
member.summary: Asynchronously writes the lines without headers and returns the result. Column count is determined from the first data line.
member.param: The lines with data that should be written.
The separator to use between columns (comma, semicolon, tab, ...)

member.name: M:Csv.CsvWriter.WriteToTextAsync(System.String[],System.Collections.Generic.IAsyncEnumerable{System.String[]},System.Char,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Writes the lines and return the result.
member.param: The headers that should be used for the first line, determines the number of columns. Can be null if skipHeaderRow is true.
The lines with data that should be written.
The separator to use between columns (comma, semicolon, tab, ...)

member.summary: Asynchronously writes the lines to the writer using memory-efficient ReadOnlyMemory<char> arrays.

The separator to use between columns (comma, semicolon, tab, ...)
Indicate whether the header row should be skipped, defaults to false.

member.name: M:Csv.CsvWriter.WriteToTextAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IAsyncEnumerable{System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Char}[]},System.Char)
member.summary: Asynchronously writes the lines without headers and returns the result using memory-efficient operations. Column count is determined from the first data line.
member.param: The lines with data that should be written.
The separator to use between columns (comma, semicolon, tab, ...)

member.name: M:Csv.CsvWriter.WriteToTextAsync(System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Char}[],System.Collections.Generic.IAsyncEnumerable{System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Char}[]},System.Char,System.Boolean)

The separator to use between columns (comma, semicolon, tab, ...)
Indicate whether the header row should be skipped, defaults to false.

member.name: T:Csv.HeaderMode
member.summary: Indicates the presence (default) or absence of a header row.

member.name: F:Csv.HeaderMode.HeaderPresent
member.summary: Indicates that the CSV file has a header row. (Default)

member.name: F:Csv.HeaderMode.HeaderAbsent
member.summary: Indicates that the CSV file does not have a header row.

member.name: T:Csv.ICsvLine
member.summary: Represents a single data line inside a csv file.

member.name: P:Csv.ICsvLine.Headers
member.summary: Gets the headers from the csv file.

member.name: P:Csv.ICsvLine.Values
member.summary: Gets a list of values in string format for the current row.

member.name: P:Csv.ICsvLine.Raw
member.summary: Gets the original raw content of the line.

member.name: P:Csv.ICsvLine.Index
member.summary: Gets the 1-based index for the line inside the file.

member.name: P:Csv.ICsvLine.ColumnCount
member.summary: Gets the number of columns of the line.

member.name: M:Csv.ICsvLine.HasColumn(System.String)
member.summary: Indicates whether the specified exists.

member.name: M:Csv.ICsvLine.LineHasColumn(System.String)
member.summary: Indicates whether the specified exists and the current line contains a value for it.

member.name: P:Csv.ICsvLine.Item(System.String)
member.summary: Gets the data for the specified named header.
member.param: The name of the header.

member.name: P:Csv.ICsvLine.Item(System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets the data for the specified indexed header.
member.param: The index of the header.

member.name: T:Csv.ICsvLineFromMemory
member.summary: Represents a single data line inside a csv file.

member.name: P:Csv.ICsvLineFromMemory.Headers
member.summary: Gets the headers from the csv file.

member.name: P:Csv.ICsvLineFromMemory.Values
member.summary: Gets a list of values in string format for the current row.

member.name: P:Csv.ICsvLineFromMemory.Raw
member.summary: Gets the original raw content of the line.

member.name: P:Csv.ICsvLineFromMemory.Index
member.summary: Gets the 1-based index for the line inside the file.

member.name: P:Csv.ICsvLineFromMemory.ColumnCount
member.summary: Gets the number of columns of the line.

member.name: M:Csv.ICsvLineFromMemory.HasColumn(System.String)
member.summary: Indicates whether the specified exists.

member.name: M:Csv.ICsvLineFromMemory.LineHasColumn(System.String)
member.summary: Indicates whether the specified exists and the current line contains a value for it.

member.name: P:Csv.ICsvLineFromMemory.Item(System.String)
member.summary: Gets the data for the specified named header.
member.param: The name of the header.

member.name: P:Csv.ICsvLineFromMemory.Item(System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets the data for the specified indexed header.
member.param: The index of the header.

member.name: T:Csv.ICsvLineSpan
member.summary: Enhanced CSV line interface with Span/Memory support for zero-allocation data access.

member.name: P:Csv.ICsvLineSpan.HeadersMemory
member.summary: Gets the headers as ReadOnlyMemory<char> for efficient memory access.

member.name: P:Csv.ICsvLineSpan.ValuesMemory
member.summary: Gets a list of values as ReadOnlyMemory<char> for the current row.

member.name: P:Csv.ICsvLineSpan.RawMemory
member.summary: Gets the original raw content of the line as ReadOnlyMemory<char>.

member.name: P:Csv.ICsvLineSpan.HeadersSpan
member.summary: Gets the headers as ReadOnlySpan<char> for zero-allocation access.

member.name: P:Csv.ICsvLineSpan.ValuesSpan
member.summary: Gets the values as ReadOnlySpan<char> for zero-allocation access.

member.name: P:Csv.ICsvLineSpan.RawSpan
member.summary: Gets the raw content as ReadOnlySpan<char> for zero-allocation access.

member.name: M:Csv.ICsvLineSpan.GetMemory(System.String)
member.summary: Gets the data for the specified named header as ReadOnlyMemory<char>.
member.param: The name of the header.

member.name: M:Csv.ICsvLineSpan.GetMemory(System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets the data for the specified column index as ReadOnlyMemory<char>.
member.param: The zero-based index of the column.

member.name: M:Csv.ICsvLineSpan.GetSpan(System.String)
member.summary: Gets the data for the specified named header as ReadOnlySpan<char>.
member.param: The name of the header.

member.name: M:Csv.ICsvLineSpan.GetSpan(System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets the data for the specified column index as ReadOnlySpan<char>.
member.param: The zero-based index of the column.

member.name: M:Csv.ICsvLineSpan.TryGetMemory(System.String,System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Char}@)
member.summary: Tries to get the data for the specified named header as ReadOnlyMemory<char>.
member.param: The name of the header.
When this method returns, contains the memory for the column if found; otherwise, empty.
member.returns: true if the column was found; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:Csv.ICsvLineSpan.TryGetMemory(System.Int32,System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Char}@)
member.summary: Tries to get the data for the specified column index as ReadOnlyMemory<char>.
member.param: The zero-based index of the column.
When this method returns, contains the memory for the column if found; otherwise, empty.
member.returns: true if the column was found; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:Csv.ICsvLineSpan.TryGetSpan(System.String,System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char}@)
member.summary: Tries to get the data for the specified named header as ReadOnlySpan<char>.
member.param: The name of the header.
When this method returns, contains the span for the column if found; otherwise, empty.
member.returns: true if the column was found; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:Csv.ICsvLineSpan.TryGetSpan(System.Int32,System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char}@)
member.summary: Tries to get the data for the specified column index as ReadOnlySpan<char>.
member.param: The zero-based index of the column.
When this method returns, contains the span for the column if found; otherwise, empty.
member.returns: true if the column was found; otherwise, false.

member.name: T:Csv.StringHelpers
member.summary: Extension methods for to handle common string operations.

member.name: M:Csv.StringHelpers.StartsWith(System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Char},System.String)
member.summary: Checks whether starts with .

member.name: M:Csv.StringHelpers.EndsWith(System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Char},System.String)
member.summary: Checks whether ends with .

member.name: M:Csv.StringHelpers.AsString(System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Char})
member.summary: Gets a value for .
